solution.p: We will factor 81t^{2}y^{2}-18ty+1 where a=81, b=-18, and c=1.

Perfect Square Formula: The formula for perfect square trinomials is (a \pm b)^2=a^2 \pm 2ab + b^2. Note that the polynomial 81t^{2}y^{2}-18ty+1 matches the formula's right side, and we have: \begin{aligned}[t]81t^{2}y^{2}-18ty+1\amp =\left(9ty\right)^2-2\left(9ty\right)\left(1\right)+\left(1\right)^2\newline \amp =\left(9ty-1\right)\mathopen{}\left(9ty-1\right)\newline \amp =\left(9ty-1\right)^{2}\end{aligned}
AC Method: In this method, we first calculate the product of a and c in ax^2+bx+c. For this problem, we have (81) \cdot (1) = 81.

The number 81 can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways:  egin{array}{lll}1(81)\amp -1(-81)\amp 3(27)\amp -3(-27)
ewline9(9)\amp -9(-9)\amp \end{array} Looking at the corresponding sums, we want a sum of -18, and this sum happens with (-9)+(-9)=-18 So we write  egin{aligned}[t]81t^{2}y^{2}-18ty+1\amp =81t^{2}y^{2}-9ty-9ty+1
ewline \amp =9ty\mathopen{}\left(9ty-1ight)-\left(9ty-1ight)
ewline \amp =\left(9ty-1ight)\mathopen{}\left(9ty-1ight)
ewline \amp =\left(9ty-1ight)^{2}\end{aligned}Generic Rectangles Method: First, we set up generic rectangles by putting the first term and third term in 81t^{2}y^{2}-18ty+1 into the top left and bottom right rectangles:

The first term's coefficient, 81, can be factored into the product of two numbers (where the first factor is positive) in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(81)\amp 3(27)\amp 9(9)\amp \end{array}
The third term, 1, can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(1)\amp -1(-1)\amp \end{array}

We put each pair into the corresponding places next to those generic rectangles, and try to match the area of those rectangles with 81t^{2}y^{2}-18ty+1 by guess-and-check. The following generic rectangles show the solution:

Dimensions of those generic rectangles in the diagram give us the solution: \begin{aligned}[t]81t^{2}y^{2}-18ty+1\amp =\left(9ty-1\right)\mathopen{}\left(9ty-1\right)\newline \amp =\left(9ty-1\right)^{2}\end{aligned}
